etymology.description: 小(小) (xiǎo, "small") + 孩(孩) (hái, "child") = small child -> kid
etymology.interpretation: This combination makes sense because 小(小) (xiǎo, "small") describes the physical size or age, and 孩(孩) (hái, "child") denotes a young person. Together, 小孩(小孩) (xiǎohái, "child; kid") is used to refer to a young child, emphasizing smallness and youth.

examples.chinese: 那个小孩很可爱(那個小孩很可愛)。
小孩喜欢听故事(小孩喜歡聽故事)。
examples.english: Children like to listen to stories.

examples.chinese: 公园里有很多小孩(公園裡有很多小孩)，他们在快乐地玩耍(他們在快樂地玩耍)。
examples.pinyin: Gōngyuán lǐ yǒu hěn duō xiǎohái, tāmen zài kuàilè de wánshuǎ.
examples.english: There are many kids in the park, and they are playing happily.
